title: Bounded state store: context-safe state files by construction (generalize watchdog bounded-index + day-partitioned archive)
source: hackathon-pm 2026-05-30: /pm-day state reload measured ~34k tokens/iteration, 26k of it unbounded-growing history (loop_a regime_history 16k + full backlog INDEX 10k). CEO directed a solid spec-first design over a cheap read-trim.
why: State files agents read each iteration grow without bound (loop_a_state.json regime_history +1/tick, backlog INDEX +1/B-ID, equity_curve +1/min). The project already solved this for watchdog_state.json (fixed-size bounded index + day-partitioned archive) but ad-hoc for one consumer. Generalize into a reusable execution/state_store.py and apply to loop_a (biggest win, 16k->1.5k) + equity curve rotation + PM active-only backlog load. Invariant: no file an agent reads into context may grow unbounded.
value_rationale: High: every /pm-day and loop-a-tick iteration pays the unbounded reload; cost grows with project history. Bounds reload ~34k->~12k tokens/iteration, constant thereafter. Durable convention, not a patch.
scope: loop_a_state.json v2 bounded shape + lru_by_cell projection + record_tick rewrite + _regime_lru_pick O(24)
Equity curve rotation+rollup (parquet source-of-truth, bounded JSON tail)
PM/skill: db.list_active() instead of full INDEX.json
recommendation: Spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-30-bounded-state-store-design.md. Rollout: state_store -> watchdog refactor -> loop_a migration -> PM docs -> equity rotation. Each step independently shippable, suite green. TDD with LRU parity test as correctness anchor.
out_of_scope: Not deleting history (archive is permanent). Not changing Loop A research behavior or LRU pick result. Not migrating backlog storage (db.list_active already exists).
